prerequisites: A Tavus account with access to the PAL Maker dashboard.
A Tavus API key generated from the PAL Maker (used to authorize every request).
A replication start date to provide for the connector's required start_date config field, which bounds how far back data is pulled.

authMethods.name: API key (x-api-key header)
authMethods.summary: Tavus authenticates every request with an API key sent in the x-api-key HTTP header. This maps to the connector's required api_key secret field. Generate the key in the PAL Maker (API Key in the sidebar), give it a name, optionally restrict it to allowed IP addresses, then copy and store it securely. Tavus advises loading the key from a server-side configuration and never exposing it in client-side code.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Tavus API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the PAL Maker and select API Key from the sidebar menu. Click Create New Key, enter a name for the key, and optionally specify allowed IP addresses. Click Create API Key, then copy and securely store the generated key. Requests authenticate by sending this key in the x-api-key header against the Tavus API base URL https://tavusapi.com/v2.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the connector's required api_key field to the key you generated in the PAL Maker. This value is the secret used for the x-api-key header on every Tavus API request, so keep it confidential and supply it via secure configuration rather than hardcoding it.

setupSteps.title: Set the replication start date
setupSteps.body: Set the connector's required start_date field to the earliest date from which records should be replicated. The connector uses this value to bound the initial pull of historical data.
